metadata.description: Curated reference papers for ML-based Groundwater Potential Mapping thesis (Pothohar Plateau, Pakistan)
metadata.note: cite_key = suggested BibTeX key. Use 'category' to map papers to thesis chapters.

categories.foundational_ml: Core ML algorithm papers (cite in Methodology)
categories.gw_potential_mapping: Groundwater potential zone mapping studies (Literature Review + Methodology)
categories.ml_hydrology_review: Review papers on ML in water/hydrology (Introduction + Literature Review)
categories.data_sources: Datasets and remote sensing platforms (Methodology - Data)
categories.pakistan_pothohar: Pakistan / Pothohar / Punjab regional studies (Study Area + Discussion)

papers.title: XGBoost: A Scalable Tree Boosting System
papers.authors: Tianqi Chen, Carlos Guestrin
papers.description: The original XGBoost paper. Cite this when introducing the XGBoost algorithm (your best model).

papers.title: Classification and Regression by randomForest
papers.authors: Andy Liaw, Matthew C. Wiener
papers.description: Widely-cited Random Forest implementation reference. Cite when describing the RF model.

papers.title: A Brief Review of Random Forests for Water Scientists and Practitioners and Their Recent History in Water Resources
papers.authors: Hristos Tyralis, Georgia Papacharalampous, Andreas Langousis
papers.description: Review of RF specifically in water resources. Strong citation linking RF to hydrology.

papers.title: The advantages of the Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC) over F1 score and accuracy in binary classification evaluation
papers.authors: Davide Chicco, Giuseppe Jurman
papers.description: Evaluation-metrics reference. Cite when justifying use of F1 / Cohen's kappa / accuracy metrics.

papers.title: GIS and AHP Techniques Based Delineation of Groundwater Potential Zones: a case study from Southern Western Ghats, India
papers.authors: P. Arulbalaji, D. Padmalal, K. Sreelash
papers.description: Highly-cited GIS+AHP GWPZ delineation. Key literature-review citation for weighted-overlay baseline.

papers.title: GIS-based groundwater potential mapping in Shahroud plain, Iran. A comparison among statistical (bivariate and multivariate), data mining and MCDM approaches
papers.authors: Alireza Arabameri, Khalil Rezaei, Artemi Cerda et al.
papers.description: Compares statistical, data-mining and MCDM GWPM methods — directly supports your ML-vs-overlay framing.

papers.title: Modeling Groundwater Potential Zone in a Semi-Arid Region of Aseer Using Fuzzy-AHP and Geoinformation Techniques
papers.authors: Javed Mallick, Roohul Abad Khan, Mohd. Ahmed et al.
papers.description: Semi-arid GWPZ modeling (same climate class as Pothohar). Good study-area analogue.

papers.title: Integration of different influencing factors in GIS to delineate groundwater potential areas using IF and FR techniques: a study of Pravara basin, Maharashtra, India
papers.authors: Sumit Das, Sudhakar D. Pardeshi
papers.description: Information Factor + Frequency Ratio GWPZ. Supports multi-factor overlay methodology.

papers.title: Mapping of Groundwater Potential Zones in Crystalline Terrain Using Remote Sensing, GIS Techniques, and Multicriteria Data Analysis (Ighrem Region, Western Anti-Atlas, Morocco)
papers.authors: Khalid Benjmel, Fouad Amraoui, Said Boutaleb et al.
papers.description: Crystalline / hard-rock terrain GWPZ — relevant to your Salt Range / Kala Chitta hard-rock discussion.

papers.title: Delineation of groundwater potential zones for sustainable development and planning using analytical hierarchy process (AHP), and MIF techniques
papers.authors: Chaitanya B. Pande, Kanak N. Moharir, Balamurugan Panneerselvam et al.
papers.description: AHP + Multi-Influencing Factor GWPZ in semi-arid India. Recent overlay-method reference.

papers.title: A comprehensive review of deep learning applications in hydrology and water resources
papers.authors: Muhammed Sit, Bekir Zahit Demiray, Zhongrun Xiang et al.
papers.description: Broad ML/DL-in-hydrology review. Use in Introduction to position the field.

papers.title: Groundwater level prediction using machine learning models: A comprehensive review
papers.authors: Tao Hai, Mohammed Majeed Hameed, Haydar Abdulameer Marhoon et al.
papers.description: Review of ML for groundwater level. Directly on-topic for Literature Review.

papers.title: A review of the application of machine learning in water quality evaluation
papers.authors: Mengyuan Zhu, Jiawei Wang, Xiao Yang et al.
papers.description: ML in water resources review. Supporting citation for data-driven water modeling.

papers.title: Assessing the predictive capability of ensemble tree methods for landslide susceptibility mapping using XGBoost, gradient boosting machine, and random forest
papers.authors: Emrehan Kutlug Sahin
papers.description: Direct XGBoost-vs-GBM-vs-RF comparison for spatial susceptibility mapping. Strong methodological analogue to your model comparison.

papers.title: Evaluating scale effects of topographic variables in landslide susceptibility models using GIS-based machine learning techniques
papers.authors: Kuan-Tsung Chang, Abdelaziz Merghadi, Ali P. Yunus et al.
papers.description: DEM resolution + topographic variables in GIS-ML. Supports your SRTM/slope/TWI feature choices.

papers.title: Application of alternating decision tree with AdaBoost and bagging ensembles for landslide susceptibility mapping
papers.authors: Yanli Wu, Yutian Ke, Zhuo Chen et al.
papers.description: Boosting + bagging ensemble spatial mapping. Supports your Voting Ensemble approach.

papers.title: Landslide susceptibility mapping using knowledge driven statistical models in Darjeeling District, West Bengal, India
papers.authors: Jagabandhu Roy, Sunil Saha
papers.description: Knowledge-driven statistical labeling — directly justifies your weighted-overlay label generation.

papers.title: Influence of Data Splitting on Performance of Machine Learning Models in Prediction of Shear Strength of Soil
papers.authors: Quang Hung Nguyen, Hai-Bang Ly, Lanh Si Ho et al.
papers.description: Train/val/test split effects on ML performance. Cite to justify your 72/8/20 split.

papers.title: SoilGrids 2.0: producing soil information for the globe with quantified spatial uncertainty
papers.authors: Laura Poggio, Luis Moreira de Sousa, N.H. Batjes et al.
papers.description: Official SoilGrids reference. MUST cite — your #1 feature (soil texture) comes from this dataset.

papers.title: Current status of Landsat program, science, and applications
papers.authors: Michael A. Wulder, Thomas R. Loveland, David P. Roy et al.
papers.description: Landsat program reference. Cite for NDVI/NDWI data source (Landsat-8).

papers.title: Rapid groundwater decline and some cases of recovery in aquifers globally
papers.authors: Scott Jasechko, Hansjorg Seybold, Debra Perrone et al.
papers.description: High-impact Nature paper on global groundwater decline. Perfect opening citation for Introduction motivation.

papers.title: Modelling of land use and land cover changes and prediction using CA-Markov and Random Forest
papers.authors: Muhammad Asif, Jamil Hasan Kazmi, Aqil Tariq et al.
papers.description: RF for LULC in Punjab (Cholistan/Thal deserts). Pakistan ML + RS regional study.

papers.title: Spatiotemporal Analysis of Drought and Agriculture Standardized Residual Yield Series Nexuses across Punjab, Pakistan
papers.authors: Muhammad Waseem, Ali Hasan Jaffry, Muhammad Azam et al.
papers.description: Drought + agriculture across Punjab. Context for water stress in study area.

papers.title: Shifting of Meteorological to Hydrological Drought Risk at Regional Scale
papers.authors: Awais Naeem Sarwar, Muhammad Waseem, Muhammad Azam et al.
papers.description: Soan basin (Pothohar arid region) drought study. Directly in your study area.

papers.title: Irrigation Supply and Demand, Land Use/Cover Change and Future Projections of Climate, in Indus Basin Irrigation System, Pakistan
papers.authors: Naveed Ahmed, Haishen Lu, Shakeel Ahmed et al.
papers.description: Indus Basin (Chaj Doab) irrigation + LULC. Regional water-resource context.

papers.title: Intercomparison and Assessment of Stand-Alone and Wavelet-Coupled Machine Learning Models for Simulating Rainfall-Runoff Process in Four Basins of Pothohar Region, Pakistan
papers.authors: Muhammad Tariq Khan, Muhammad Shoaib, Raffaele Albano et al.
papers.description: ML rainfall-runoff in FOUR POTHOHAR BASINS. Very close regional + methodological match — high priority cite.

papers.title: Mapping of potential storages and rainwater harvesting sites in arid region of Indus basin using analytical hierarchy technique
papers.authors: Muhammad Hafeez, Muhammad Jehanzeb Masud Cheema, Umar Waqas Liaqat et al.
papers.description: AHP water-storage mapping in Pothowar region (same area). Recent, directly comparable methodology.

papers.title: Water quality assessment and trends in twin cities of Pakistan: a review
papers.authors: Kashaf Waseem, Abdul Saboor Akhtar, Ahsan Nawaz et al.
papers.description: Rawalpindi/Islamabad twin-cities water quality review. Local groundwater context for validation discussion.

papers.title: Analysis of Constraining Factors Regarding Adoption of Soil and Water Conservation Practices in the Pothohar, Punjab
papers.authors: Raza Ejaz, Khalid Mahmood Chaudhary, Ijaz Ashraf et al.
papers.description: Soil + water conservation in Pothohar (Rawalpindi, max rainfall). Socio-physical context.

papers.title: Evaluating potential of dry afforestation techniques on barren land in Attock
papers.authors: Shehryar Khan, Sabeeqa Usman Malik, Saeed Gulzar et al.
papers.description: Attock district (study area) barren-land / semi-arid context. Supports land-degradation discussion.

papers.title: Assessing the impact of socio-demographic factors on municipal water security in planned and unplanned urban centers of Pakistan
papers.authors: Kamran, Umer Khayyam, Fasiha Safdar et al.
papers.venue: AQUA - Water Infrastructure Ecosystems and Society
papers.description: Pakistan municipal water security. Socio-economic motivation for the study.

papers.title: The significance of morphometric analysis to understand the hydrological and morphological characteristics in two different morpho-climatic settings
papers.authors: Avijit Mahala
papers.description: Morphometric/drainage analysis for hydrology. Supports terrain-feature rationale.

papers.title: Hydrological inferences through morphometric analysis of lower Kosi river basin of India for water resource management based on remote sensing data
papers.authors: Praveen Kumar, Rajeev Singh Chandel, Varun Narayan Mishra et al.
papers.description: RS-based drainage morphometry for groundwater prospects. Supports drainage-density / terrain features.

papers.title: Flood Susceptibility Mapping through the GIS-AHP Technique Using the Cloud
papers.authors: Kishore Chandra Swain, Chiranjit Singha, Laxmikanta Nayak
papers.description: Cloud-based GIS-AHP susceptibility mapping. Methodological analogue for multi-criteria spatial analysis.
